title: feat(senseaudio): add SenseAudio TTS provider
author: KLilyZ
body:
## Summary
- Problem: `senseaudio` bundled plugin only has ASR; no TTS.
- Why it matters: completes the round trip in the same plugin.
- What changed: registers a `speechProvider` in `extensions/senseaudio/`. Synthesizes via `/v1/t2a_v2`, lists system voices via `/v1/get_voice`, transcodes MP3 to opus for voice-note targets.

## Validation
- Targeted extension tests passed.
- Contract plugin tests passed.

## Scope
Telephony, streaming, voice cloning, and text-generated voices are intentionally out of scope.

activity.author: github-actions
activity.body:
### Dependency Changes Detected
This PR changes dependency-related files. Maintainers should confirm these changes are intentional.

Changed files:
- `extensions/senseaudio/package.json`

activity.author: osolmaz
activity.body: Summary: The PR adds a SenseAudio speech provider to the bundled senseaudio plugin, with TTS HTTP helpers, voice listing, tests, and docs. Needs maintainer review before merge.

activity.author: KLilyZ
activity.body: Updated the provider docs and added coverage for voice listing, synthesis failures, and opus transcoding. The remaining files are intentionally scoped to the bundled extension.

activity.author: osolmaz
activity.body: Codex review: needs changes before merge. Please keep the SenseAudio provider surface narrow, verify the voice listing error path, and make sure the new speech provider file is covered by targeted extension tests. After the follow-up, all bot checks have enough detail for maintainers to inspect status without opening the browser.

activity.author: KLilyZ
activity.body: Follow-up note: voice list failures now surface the provider error, synthesis failures include the SenseAudio status code, and opus conversion is covered with a fixture-backed test.

activity.author: github-actions
activity.body: marked all required checks as passing
